evals.name: detroit-mini-canon
evals.prompt: I want a small research-grade playlist doc: 2 notable Detroit techno tracks per year for 1991-1993 (6 total), and every track must have core involvement from a Black Detroit artist. Use discogs.com as the source of truth. Deliver a markdown table sorted by release date with: Artist and Track EXACTLY as credited on the Discogs release page, a hard link to that release page, a YouTube link taken from the videos embedded on that same Discogs page (validated as live), notes on why each track is notable, and a status column. Also produce a playlist_items.json with the YouTube video ids in chronological order. My Discogs token is in TOKENS_DIR/.discogs_token and my YouTube API key is in TOKENS_DIR/.yt_key. Write outputs to OUTPUT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: detroit-mini.md (or similar) with 6 verified rows + playlist_items.json with validated video ids
assertions: Markdown table has 6 rows with Discogs release links
Every Discogs link resolves to a real release via the API
Artist cell matches the release-page credit exactly (join phrases included)
Track cell matches a title on the release's tracklist verbatim
Every YouTube pick comes from the release page's videos, or the deviation/skip is noted in the row
playlist_items.json exists and every video id is live and public
Rows are sorted ascending by release date
All six tracks credibly involve Black Detroit artists (grader judgment)

evals.name: dance-mania-label-retro
evals.prompt: Make me a mini label retrospective: 5 essential Dance Mania (the Chicago label) releases from 1994-1996, max one per artist. Source of truth is discogs.com — I want a markdown table sorted by release date: artist + track exactly as credited on the Discogs release page, hard Discogs release link, a YouTube link picked from the videos on that Discogs release page (skip an entry's video if the page has none, note it), why-it-matters notes, and per-row status. Plus a playlist_items.json of the validated YouTube ids in order. Discogs token: TOKENS_DIR/.discogs_token; YouTube key: TOKENS_DIR/.yt_key. Put everything in OUTPUT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: 5-row verified table + playlist_items.json; YouTube picks traceable to Discogs page videos or honestly skipped
assertions: Markdown table has 5 rows with Discogs release links
Every Discogs link resolves to a real release via the API
Artist cell matches the release-page credit exactly (join phrases included)
Track cell matches a title on the release's tracklist verbatim
Every YouTube pick comes from the release page's videos, or the deviation/skip is noted in the row
playlist_items.json exists and every video id is live and public
Rows are sorted ascending by release date
Every release is on the required label (Dance Mania)
Max one release per artist (all artists distinct)
